Design Process Authenticity Evaluation

Domain: {{ participantDomain }} Project {{ projectIndex + 1 }} / {{ domainProjects.length }} Overall {{ overallProgress }}%

Welcome to the Design Process Evaluation Study

You will review real design-process records from your own design domain and judge how authentic each phase feels, and which cognitive states the designer was experiencing.

What You'll Do:

  • Review design projects from your domain, one project at a time
  • For each phase, rate how authentic the design process feels (1–7)
  • Identify the cognitive states present in each phase
  • Give an overall judgment for each completed project

Understanding Cognitive States in Design

As you evaluate each phase, you'll identify which of these four cognitive states the designer was likely experiencing. A phase may involve more than one.

Ambiguity

Multiple Interpretations

The brief or situation can be read in several valid but conflicting ways.

Vagueness

Unclear Boundaries

Requirements are fuzzy or underspecified; the edges aren't well defined.

Uncertainty

Insufficient Information

Key facts are missing or unknowable, so outcomes can't be predicted.

Tacit Knowledge

Intuitive Understanding

The designer relies on hard-to-articulate experience and intuition.
